sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Quantitative Investment Management Boosts Stake in Cooper Companies
feed.summary: Quantitative Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in The Cooper Companies, Inc. by 111.8% in the third quarter, bringing its total holdings to 36,024 shares valued at $2,469,000. This increase suggests the institutional investor's confidence in the medical device company's performance and growth prospects. Cooper Companies is a key player in vision care and women's health markets, making institutional ownership changes a strong indicator of market sentiment.

feed.title: Ag Groups Urge Mosaic, J.R. Simplot to Renounce Fertilizer Duties
feed.summary: A coalition of 64 agricultural groups, led by the National Corn Growers Association, has called on Mosaic Company and J.R. Simplot to advocate for the removal of duties on imported phosphate products from Morocco. Citing the impact of the Middle East conflict and already high U.S. input prices, the groups argue that these duties contribute to increased fertilizer costs and shortages, negatively affecting U.S. agricultural security. The duties were initially imposed in 2020 following a petition by Mosaic, which was supported by J.R. Simplot, alleging unfair foreign subsidies.

feed.title: Applied Materials Lifts Dividend As AI Partnerships Shape Shareholder Returns
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has increased its quarterly cash dividend by 15%, marking nine years of dividend growth, while simultaneously expanding partnerships with Micron and SK Hynix for AI memory development. The company, trading at $337.27 with significant year-to-date and annual returns, is balancing shareholder returns with substantial R&D investments in AI-focused chip production, though insider selling has been noted as a potential risk.

feed.title: Assessing BlackLine (BL) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Mixed Profitability Signals
feed.summary: BlackLine (BL) has experienced significant share price weakness, falling 20% in the last month and 39% in the past three months, despite annual revenue growth of 9.8% and net income growth of 48.8%. While a popular narrative suggests the stock is 19.2% undervalued with a fair value of $44.00, its high P/E ratio of 86.3x compared to the industry average of 28.1x points to potential valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to review the fundamentals and consider potential risks or opportunities, such as platform traction or deepening partnerships.

feed.title: Is Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) Attractively Priced After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) has experienced recent share price weakness, with a 7.2% decline in the past week. Despite this, an Excess Returns analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 57.2%, estimating an intrinsic value of $36.20 per share compared to its current price of $15.48. Its P/E ratio of 15.06x is slightly above the industry average but aligns closely with Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio, indicating it is broadly priced correctly by that measure.

feed.title: A Look At Wynn Resorts (WYNN) Valuation As Middle East Tensions Test UAE Project Prospects
feed.summary: Wynn Resorts' valuation is under scrutiny due to Middle East tensions impacting its UAE project, Wynn Al Marjan Island. The stock has seen a recent decline despite a positive 1-year return, with analysts suggesting it is undervalued by 30.5% due to the potential of its resort pipeline, though a high P/E ratio indicates some valuation risk. The article advises investors to review the numbers and consider risks like Macau regulation or the UAE project underperforming.

feed.title: BofA, Evercore, and Morgan Stanley Cut Price Targets for HP Inc. (HPQ)
feed.summary: BofA, Evercore ISI, and Morgan Stanley have all cut their price targets for HP Inc. (HPQ) following the company's recent earnings results. Analysts cited factors like slower PC unit growth, margin pressure from memory costs, leadership transition uncertainty, and a tough memory environment for their revised outlooks. Despite Morgan Stanley seeing the stock as "cheap," they forecast over 14% downside risk to fiscal year 2026 EPS guidance.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Focused on Growth through Strategic Partnerships
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) is prioritizing growth through strategic partnerships, particularly in the data center and AI markets. CEO Lisa Su highlighted this focus at a recent conference, while the company has already secured significant partnerships with Meta Platforms and OpenAI to power AI infrastructure. AMD aims for substantial AI revenue and a high compound annual growth rate in the coming years.

feed.title: Pawtucket to hold job fair for Hasbro workers, despite not knowing how many jobs will be lost
feed.summary: The city of Pawtucket plans to organize a job fair for Hasbro Inc. employees in response to the company's relocation to Boston. This initiative is moving forward despite the city not knowing the exact number of Rhode Island workers who will be displaced by the move. Hasbro has already shed 1,970 jobs globally since 2022, but has not yet informed the state about layoffs specifically tied to the Boston relocation.

feed.title: UBS and BofA Lift Price Targets on Viatris (VTRS)
feed.summary: UBS and BofA have both raised their price targets on Viatris (VTRS) following the company's cost-saving initiatives and favorable 2026 guidance. UBS maintained a Buy rating, citing expected cost-saving targets and potential for long-term growth and EPS increase, while BofA maintained an Underperform rating despite the increased price target. Viatris plans to achieve $400 million in net cost reductions over three years.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical discloses cybersecurity breach
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical disclosed a cybersecurity breach where an unauthorized third party accessed internal IT business applications due to a phishing incident. The breach compromised some customer business and contact information, as well as employee and corporate data, but did not affect its robotic surgical systems or manufacturing operations. The company is investigating the incident and has emphasized that its robotic systems have separate security protocols.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Co. Faces Financial Challenges Amid Valuation Concerns and Bearish Trends
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. is facing significant financial challenges due to shifts in its evaluation, reflecting both technical and valuation concerns. The company is experiencing a bearish trend, with indicators like MACD, Bollinger Bands, and moving averages signaling downward movement. Furthermore, Campbell Soup Co. is classified as very expensive compared to industry peers, with a high P/E ratio and enterprise value to EBITDA, raising significant valuation concerns despite decent profitability metrics.

feed.title: Insulet to spend up to $40M on Omnipod 5 pod recall - Boston Business Journal
feed.summary: Insulet Corp. is set to incur costs of up to $40 million for the recall of its Omnipod 5 tubeless insulin pump pods. The Acton-based company initiated the recall after receiving 18 reports of serious adverse events related to insufficient insulin delivery from the devices.

feed.title: American Express Sues Ray J Over $78,000 in Unpaid Credit Card Bills
feed.summary: American Express has filed a lawsuit against singer Ray J for $78,704.56 in unpaid credit card bills, alleging a breach of contract. This comes after a previous lawsuit by AmEx in November for even larger unpaid bills. Ray J is also involved in a separate and ongoing legal battle with Kim Kardashian and Kris Jenner concerning his 2007 sex tape.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) Target Price Lowered by Truist Securi
feed.summary: Truist Securities has lowered its price target for Fifth Third Bancorp (FITB) from $60.00 to $53.00, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment comes despite other analysts raising their price targets for the regional bank in recent months. The average one-year price target from 19 analysts is $57.61, suggesting a potential upside of 30.82% from its current price.

feed.title: International Paper to Sell its Global Cellulose Fibers Business
feed.summary: International Paper announced its definitive agreement to sell its Global Cellulose Fibers (GCF) business unit to American Industrial Partners for approximately $1.5 billion. This strategic move aims to sharpen the company's focus on its core operations and expanding sustainable packaging solutions. The GCF business, which generated $2.8 billion in 2024 revenue, produces fluff pulp for hygiene products and specialty pulp for industrial applications.

feed.title: SLB (SLB) Is Down 6.0% After Middle East Disruptions Cut Q1 Outlook Amid New Malaysia Win
feed.summary: SLB (SLB) experienced a 6.0% drop after announcing that first-quarter revenue would be below expectations due to Middle East disruptions, impacting earnings by US$0.06–US$0.09 per diluted share. Concurrently, its OneSubsea joint venture secured a significant deepwater subsea contract in Malaysia, highlighting the company's ability to win new projects in other regions despite geopolitical shocks. This dual development creates a nuanced investment narrative, requiring investors to weigh regional instability against new growth opportunities and long-term forecasts.

feed.title: Huntington Ingalls Gains Advantage From Robust Demand in Naval Ship Construction
feed.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) is a top U.S. defense contractor specializing in naval vessel construction, boasting a significant backlog of $53.14 billion due to robust demand and its exclusive role in building nuclear aircraft carriers. Despite strong growth drivers and recent stock outperformance, the company faces challenges including supply chain disruptions, rising material costs, and labor shortages at its Newport News Shipbuilding division. Investors might also consider Draganfly (DPRO), GE Aerospace (GE), and Howmet Aerospace (HWM) which hold Zacks Rank #2 (Buy).

feed.title: Southwest Airlines to end service at O'Hare, Dulles
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it will end service at Chicago's O'Hare International Airport and Dulles International Airport outside Washington, D.C., starting June 4. This decision is part of an ongoing effort to refine its services, with the airline maintaining robust operations at Chicago Midway, Baltimore/Washington International, and Reagan Washington National. Southwest had expanded to O'Hare in 2021, but Midway remains a key hub for the airline.

feed.title: Genuine Parts (GPC) Expects Split to Close in 9-12 Months
feed.summary: Genuine Parts Co. (NYSE: GPC) CEO Will Stengel announced that the company anticipates completing the split of its automotive and motion businesses into two independent entities within 9 to 12 months. The separation costs are expected to be manageable, potentially below initial estimates of $400 million to $500 million. This strategic split is the result of a three-year plan, and the company will focus on executing strategic initiatives for sequential EBITDA improvement this year.

feed.title: Investment Firm Criticizes CoStar (CSGP) Financial Transparency
feed.summary: D.E. Shaw, an investment firm, has criticized CoStar Group Inc. (NASDAQ:CSGP) for reconfiguring its reporting segments, combining Homes.com with other operations into a new "Residential" segment. This change reduces financial transparency, specifically by discontinuing disclosure of net new bookings for Homes.com, a metric previously vital for investors. D.E. Shaw had previously raised concerns about Homes.com's performance and argues that the reduced disclosure hinders accountability for management.

feed.title: PR News | S&P Global Remains ‘Negative’ on WPP - Fri., Mar. 13, 2026
feed.summary: S&P Global has reaffirmed its "Negative" BBB rating on WPP, citing expected challenges for the advertising giant over the next 12 months. The agency predicts WPP may not achieve organic revenue growth and profitability will remain weak due to strategic execution uncertainties. However, S&P acknowledges potential for organic growth in 2027 if CEO Cindy Rose's "Elevate28" plan, which simplifies operations, is successful, and notes recent new business wins as a positive factor for future revenue.

feed.title: J&J succeeds in trial for drug delivery system (JNJ:NYSE)
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) announced positive results from a Phase 1 trial for a novel drug delivery system. This system is designed to administer its bladder cancer therapy, erdafitinib, which was developed in collaboration with Otsuka (OTSKY) (OTSKF). The trial successfully met its primary safety endpoint.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Ending Flights At Challenging OHare
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it is discontinuing service at Chicago O'Hare International Airport and Washington Dulles, effective June 4. The company cited "challenging" operations at O'Hare but assured that its "robust" service at Chicago Midway International Airport, where it has operated for 41 years, will continue. Affected employees will have opportunities to transfer within the company's network, including to Midway for Chicago-based staff.

feed.title: BUUU Group (BUUU) CEO and Chair report control of 6,040,000 shares
feed.summary: BUUU Group CEO Poon Wai Kwong has filed a Form 3, disclosing indirect beneficial ownership of 6,040,000 Class A Ordinary Shares through BUBI Services Limited. These shares are jointly controlled by Mr. Poon and Ms. Nana Chan, the Chair of the Board, who together hold voting and dispositive power. The filing indicates significant insider ownership but does not represent a new purchase or sale of shares.

feed.title: 9 Financials Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies nine financials stocks that have triggered "whale alerts" due to unusual options activity. It explains how traders use these alerts to spot potential trading opportunities when options market estimations diverge from normal values, providing details on specific call and put option trades for companies like Webull, Fifth Third Bancorp, and PayPal. The article also defines key options terminology for better understanding.

feed.title: Director John Belk awarded 2,857 Bassett (BSET) restricted shares
feed.summary: John R. Belk, a director at Bassett Furniture Industries (BSET), was granted 2,857 restricted shares valued at $14.00 per share, based on the closing price on March 11, 2026. These shares, awarded under the 2021 Stock Incentive Plan, will vest in one year and are subject to forfeiture risk and stock ownership guidelines. Following this grant, Belk directly holds a total of 46,407 Bassett common shares.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR) Shareholders
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is reminding investors of BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR) about an upcoming March 23, 2026 deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that BellRing made misleading statements regarding its sales results, which were not due to increased consumer demand but rather customers accumulating excess inventory. This led to significant stock price drops when BellRing disclosed lowered sales expectations and competitive pressures.

feed.title: BCY.DE Barclays PLC XETRA pre-market 13 Mar: Most active at €4.55 watch support
feed.summary: Barclays PLC (BCY.DE) is the most active stock on XETRA pre-market on March 13, 2026, trading at €4.55 with exceptionally high volume due to recent ex-dividend activity and sector rotation. Despite a pre-market dip, Meyka AI forecasts significant long-term upside, with a 12-month projection of €6.99. Traders are advised to monitor support and resistance levels closely.

feed.title: Director Emily Hoffman granted 23,867 BNED shares in stock award
feed.summary: Barnes & Noble Education director Emily Hoffman was granted 23,867 restricted shares of common stock as a stock award, bringing her direct holdings to 51,738 shares. These shares have a vesting period of one year from the grant date or until the company's next annual meeting. Hoffman is also part of a Section 13(d) group with over 10% beneficial ownership of BNED, though she disclaims beneficial ownership of other members' shares.

feed.title: Barnes & Noble Education (NYSE: BNED) director adds 23,867-share grant
feed.summary: Barnes & Noble Education director William C. Martin has been granted 23,867 restricted shares of common stock at no cost. These shares will vest earlier of one year from the grant date or the company’s next annual stockholder meeting. This transaction increases his direct holdings to 169,738 shares, with additional indirect holdings through various entities, bringing his total beneficial ownership, including those affiliated with Toro 18 Holdings LLC, to a significant amount.

feed.title: Market Watch Rx: Pfizer Joins Top Gainers
feed.summary: The article "Market Watch Rx: Pfizer Joins Top Gainers" highlights Pfizer's inclusion among top-gaining companies. This update appears to be part of a market watch or financial news segment focusing on pharmaceutical companies.

feed.title: Long term debt to total assets ratio of Baidu, Inc. Shs UnSp Singapore Depositary Receipt Repr 1/10 Sh – FWB:B1C1
feed.summary: This article focuses on the long-term debt to total assets ratio for Baidu, Inc. (B1C1) on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. It provides context for financial analysis of the company's capital structure. The article itself does not present specific financial figures but highlights the category of data.

feed.title: ASML (ASML) Gained 55.8% in 2025
feed.summary: Emerald Wealth Partners' Q4 2025 investor letter for its "Focused Equity Strategy" highlighted ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) as a strong performer, gaining 55.8% in 2025 and contributing significantly to the portfolio's returns. The firm attributes ASML's bright prospects to the high demand for silicon in AI infrastructure deployment. While ASML showed strong performance, the article notes that 101 hedge funds held ASML, but suggests certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with less risk.

feed.title: Market Chatter: BHP Gets Temporary Relaxation on Restrictions by China Mineral Resources
feed.summary: BHP Group Ltd has reportedly received a temporary relaxation on restrictions imposed by China Mineral Resources. The article also touches upon Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, which showed a revenue increase of 35% year-over-year to $609 million, surpassing analyst estimates, despite reporting a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Chipotle Set to Open Newest State College Area Location
feed.summary: Chipotle is opening its third Centre County location and first with a drive-thru pickup lane at 1880 S. Atherton St. in College Township on March 18. This new "Chipotlane" facility will operate daily from 10:45 a.m. to 10 p.m. and plans for another new location near Bellefonte are in progress. The store will offer its standard menu, new high-protein items, and special rewards for college students.

feed.title: Liquid Networx Wins Two Partner of the Year Awards at Fortinet Accelerate 2026
feed.summary: Liquid Networx has been recognized with two Partner of the Year Awards at Fortinet Accelerate 2026, marking them as the first North American partner to achieve this distinction. They were named Fortinet's first Engage Preferred Services Partner (EPSP) Partner of the Year and, for the third consecutive year, the Unified SASE Partner of the Year. These awards highlight Liquid Networx's exceptional service delivery, expertise in securing hybrid workforces with Fortinet Unified SASE, and commitment to customer satisfaction.

feed.title: Is Motorola Solutions (MSI) Pricing In Too Much Growth After Recent Contract Wins?
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions (MSI) stock has seen significant gains recently, prompting questions about its valuation. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is 21.3% overvalued at its current price of $462.63, with an intrinsic value estimated at $381.29 per share. Furthermore, its P/E ratio of 35.58x is above Simply Wall St's calculated Fair Ratio of 26.12x, indicating it may be overvalued based on earnings.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial (RJF) Completes GreensLedge Acquisition
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial (NYSE: RJF) has completed its acquisition of GreensLedge Holdings LLC, a boutique investment bank specializing in structured credit and securitization. This acquisition, announced in October 2025 and finalized on March 2, 2026, aims to enhance Raymond James' capital markets capabilities and fix income offerings. Additionally, Raymond James' Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.54 per share, payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders on record by April 1, 2026.

feed.title: 5 Industrials Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies five industrials stocks that experienced significant options activity, known as "whale alerts," during today's trading session. These alerts signify large-scale options trades that could indicate unusual market sentiment. The article details the specific call and put options, expiration dates, strike prices, and underlying sentiments (bullish, bearish, neutral) for EHang Holdings, American Airlines Group, Lockheed Martin, Boeing, and Axon Enterprise.

feed.title: KKR (KKR) explores $3 billion sale of data center cooling firm CoolIT Systems
feed.summary: KKR is reportedly exploring the sale of CoolIT Systems, a data center cooling solutions provider, in a deal that could value the company at approximately $3 billion. This potential sale highlights the growing interest in infrastructure supporting the booming data center industry, particularly advanced cooling technologies crucial for high-performance computing.

feed.title: ASML Stock Climbed 83% Last Year. Here’s Why It Could Deliver 80% More Upside
feed.summary: ASML, the sole supplier of EUV lithography machines, saw its stock climb 83% last year and is predicted to deliver 80% more upside. This growth is driven by record Q4 2025 bookings, strong AI-driven demand across its customer base, and increasing sales of its most advanced products and services, leading to robust revenue and EPS growth forecasts. Wall Street analysts and TIKR’s valuation model both support a significant increase in ASML's stock price, despite potential risks related to China and High NA adoption timing.

feed.title: Tony Ciero's Top Picks: Microsoft Corp, ServiceNow & Tourmaline Oil
feed.summary: Tony Ciero, Vice President and Senior Portfolio Manager at Caldwell Securities, has shared his top stock picks. His current recommendations for investors are Microsoft Corp, ServiceNow, and Tourmaline Oil. The article also provides links to related videos featuring Ciero's previous picks and market outlooks as well as other analysts' recommendations.

feed.title: Western Digital Corp Stock (WDC) Moved Up by 5.03% on Mar 13: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Western Digital Corp (WDC) saw its stock price increase by 5.03% on March 13, driven by strong demand for high-capacity HDDs for cloud and AI workloads. The company's Q2 FY2026 earnings exceeded expectations, and management set an ambitious "Road to $20 EPS" target within three years. Positive analyst ratings, upgrades, and the company's innovation roadmap also contributed to investor confidence.

feed.title: Cardinal Health announces closure of Augusta factory, more than 200 job cuts
feed.summary: Cardinal Health is closing its KPR US LLC subsidiary in Augusta, Georgia, by September, leading to over 200 job cuts. The facility produces wound care products such as alcohol prep pads. This move follows other recent job reductions in Georgia's manufacturing sector.

feed.title: A Morgan Stanley exec flags energy security as a top investment theme right now. Here's how to play it.
feed.summary: Jitania Kandhari, deputy CIO at Morgan Stanley Investment Management, identifies energy security as a prime investment opportunity, particularly in the wake of the ongoing US-Iran war. She advises focusing on renewable energy and critical metals like copper and uranium to capitalize on countries' increasing efforts towards energy independence. Kandhari also suggests that defense and cybersecurity sectors could benefit from the enhanced security infrastructure accompanying energy initiatives.

feed.title: 12 Industrials Stocks Moving In Friday's Intraday Session
feed.summary: This article details the intraday movements of 12 industrial stocks on Friday, highlighting both gainers and losers. Notable movers include Shimmick, Commercial Vehicle Group, and Karat Packaging showing gains, while Agroz, Nocera, and Quest Resource Holding experienced declines. The report also mentions recent Q4 earnings releases for several of these companies.

feed.title: T-Mobile’s New Wireless Deals Offer Families Long-Term Savings
feed.summary: T-Mobile is offering new wireless deals, targeting families with long-term savings compared to Verizon and AT&T. According to a HarrisX study, families with more than three lines saved over $3,700 with T-Mobile in the past five years. New customers can get benefits like discounts on lines, free iPhones, unlimited talk and text, high-speed data, and included streaming services.

feed.title: Eastman CFO Willie McLain to Address J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical Company's Executive Vice President and CFO, Willie McLain, is scheduled to speak at the J.P. Morgan Industrials Conference on March 18, 2026, at 10:45 a.m. ET. A live webcast of his presentation will be available on the company's investor relations website, with an audio replay also accessible after the event. The announcement was released on March 13, 2026, from Kingsport, Tennessee.

feed.title: Short Interest in Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE:OMC) Rises By 77.8%
feed.summary: Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE:OMC) experienced a significant 77.8% increase in short interest during February, reaching 46,725,132 shares, representing 15.2% of its outstanding stock. This rise occurred alongside the company missing quarterly earnings estimates, reporting EPS of $2.59 against an expected $2.94, despite a 27.9% year-over-year revenue increase. The board authorized a $5.0 billion share buyback and declared an $0.80 quarterly dividend, yielding approximately 4.1%.
